author: y4rr
content: ADR #663 item 5 landed via architect-mode + opus 4.7 subagent (#682). Architect caught & corrected my own misframing in the pre-flight ('lgamma/digamma → opt-in fallback' was wrong; the actual §3 violation is silent CPU fallback in EVERY distribution method via data() + TensorStorage::cpu re-upload). Corrected scope: 27 distribution files + 12 kl_* functions + 5 transforms + TransformedDistribution::log_prob → 169 methods now guarded by shared helper crate::fallback::check_gpu_fallback_opt_in. Helper: env-gated (FERROTORCH_ENABLE_GPU_FALLBACK), default Err(NotImplementedOnCuda), opt-in path emits tracing::warn! per call. CPU compute bodies UNCHANGED (correct). Cargo additions: tracing.workspace=true, ferrotorch-gpu optional dep in [dependencies] (not [dev-deps] — applied item-8 lesson), cuda feature gate. 5 fallback module tests PASS on real CUDA. 365 lib tests --features cuda PASS, 362 lib tests no-features PASS (no regressions). clippy -D warnings PASS, workspace build PASS. NO GPU code added (correctly out of scope; that's Category A). Architect-mode trial round 2: cycle was clean 1-round (no REDIRECT needed) — opus 4.7 nailed it on first dispatch. Compares favorably to item 8 (sonnet, needed 1 redirect for Cargo.toml syntax). Two data points; protocol shortens cycles. ADR #663 status: items 1-8 all DONE (item 5 was last). Remaining tracked work: #677 (jit rustdoc pass for ~150 pub items) — not in ADR #663, separate. ADR #663 fully closed.
